area_description: Agentic AI refers to LLM-based systems that can autonomously plan, reason, and take actions to accomplish tasks.

categories.name: Multi-call Tool Use with Fixed Plan
categories.description: The model generates a plan for a task, then executes the plan by calling tools. The plan is fixed and cannot be modified.

sub_topics.name: Tool Profiling
sub_topics.description: Generate detailed profiles to guide LLMs for right tool call and right parameterization.

sub_topics.name: Tool-use Post-training
sub_topics.description: Post-train the LLM to understand tool profiles and generate tool calls.

categories.name: Multi-call Tool Use with Flexible Plan
categories.description: The model generates a plan for a task, then executes the plan by calling tools. The plan can be modified based on the returned results.

sub_topics.name: Invoking Internalized APIs
sub_topics.description: APIs are either well understood (web search, calculator), or internalized into the parameters such as through embeddings.

sub_topics.name: Web Agents
sub_topics.description: Operate web browser for browsing, linking, searching, button clicking.

sub_topics.name: Prompt-based Optimization
sub_topics.description: Optimize which tools to use next based on previous tool-calling output, such as with contrastive learning and checking at critical points.

sub_topics.name: RL-based Tool Use
sub_topics.description: Optimize tool use through reinforcement learning. The reward function can be final tool call success, turn-level success, tool call correctness, etc.

sub_topics.name: Reflection-based Tool Use
sub_topics.description: Compare tool-call with expert actions to learn why expert actions are better.

categories.name: Multi-turn with User Interactions
categories.description: Based on user responses, generate next steps. One challenge is to help users resolve the ambiguity and make decisions, balancing exploration and exploitation.

categories.name: Multi-agent
categories.description: Use multiple agents to collaboratively fulfill tasks. There are various ways including role-based, lead-worker, and decentralized approaches.

sub_topics.name: Role-based Multi-agent
sub_topics.description: Different agents play different roles to collaboratively fulfill tasks.

sub_topics.name: Lead-Worker Multi-agent
sub_topics.description: There is a lead agent that orchestrates and worker agents that execute subtasks.

sub_topics.name: Decentralized Multi-agent
sub_topics.description: Decentralized agents work and collaborate without a central coordinator.

categories.name: Multi-task Planning
categories.description: There is not a single task, but oftentimes a big task with multiple subtasks, such as planning a day instead of one single activity.

categories.name: Agent Evolution
categories.description: The agent will gradually evolve to become more capable over time.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply the techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
